chapter_title: About the Desirability of Shaking Hands

hadith_entries.text:
454. Narrated Al-Bara'a bin Azib: 'The Messenger of
Allah said: "There are no two Muslims who meet and
shake hands except that Allah forgives them before
parting."""
بْنِ

455. Narrated Anas ibn Malik: ‘A man said: "Messenger
of Allah! If a man meets his brother or his friend is he
permitted to bow (to greet him)?" The Messenger of Allah
said: "No." The man said: "Is he permitted to embrace and
kiss him?" The Messenger of Allah said: "No." The man
said: "Is he permitted to take his hand and shake hands with
him?" The Messenger of Allah said: "Yes."""

456. Narrated Qatada: 'I said to Anas ibn Malik: "Did the
companions of the Messenger of Allah used to shake hands
with each other?" He said; "Yes."

457. Narrated Abu-Omama: 'The Prophet said: "Putting
one's hand on the forehead of the diseased perfects his visit
and shaking with the hand perfects the greeting."""
*
